comment: Structured known-issue records used by the wizard, device pages, and troubleshooting index. confidence uses ADL evidence levels; severity 'blocker' stops the path, 'high' produces a required warning.

issues.title: Samsung Auto Blocker prevents installing the Termux:X11 APK
symptoms: Installation of the Termux:X11 APK is silently blocked or shows an Auto Blocker notice on One UI 6+
issues.cause: One UI 6 ships Auto Blocker enabled by default; it blocks app installs from 'unauthorized sources' including sideloaded APKs.
diagnostics: Settings > Security and privacy > Auto Blocker — check whether it is On
issues.safeFix: Temporarily disable Auto Blocker, install the official Termux:X11 APK, then re-enable Auto Blocker. Do not leave it disabled.
issues.rollback: Re-enable Auto Blocker in Settings after installation.
issues.securityImpact: Temporarily reduces install-source protection; restore immediately after installing.

issues.title: Termux add-on signature mismatch between install sources
symptoms: Installing Termux:X11 or another add-on fails with 'App not installed' / signatures do not match previously installed version
issues.cause: Termux builds from F-Droid, GitHub, and Google Play are signed with different keys; the app and all add-ons must come from the same source.
diagnostics: Check where Termux was installed from; TERMUX_APK_RELEASE inside Termux reports the release source
issues.safeFix: Back up your data (proot-distro backup), fully uninstall Termux and all companion apps, then reinstall everything from one source (F-Droid recommended).
issues.securityImpact: None beyond reinstalling from the official source.

issues.title: Android 12+ kills background Termux processes (signal 9)
symptoms: [Process completed (signal 9) - press Enter] appears in Termux
Desktop session dies when Termux goes to the background
issues.cause: Android 12+ limits 'phantom' child processes (default cap 32 system-wide) and kills background children using excessive CPU.
diagnostics: Session dies without an error while Termux is backgrounded; Termux shows signal 9
issues.safeFix: Exempt Termux from battery optimization and keep it in the foreground (notification pinned) during long installs.
issues.workaround: Advanced (ADB, reversible): on Android 12L/13+, 'adb shell settings put global settings_enable_monitor_phantom_procs false'. On Android 12.0, raise max_phantom_processes via device_config with sync disabled. Both are optional, documented with risks, and never run automatically by ADL.
issues.rollback: Re-run the same settings commands with the default values (or reset developer options).
issues.securityImpact: Disabling device_config sync also disables RescueParty auto-recovery until re-enabled; re-enable before OS updates.

sources.title: Android-Docs — phantom, cached and empty processes

issues.title: No sound in the Linux desktop until PulseAudio forwarding is configured
symptoms: Desktop launches but applications play no audio
Volume controls in the desktop appear disconnected
issues.cause: Sound must be forwarded from the Linux environment to a PulseAudio server running in Termux; installing a desktop or PulseAudio inside the distro alone does nothing.
issues.safeFix: Run PulseAudio in Termux with the localhost-only TCP module and set PULSE_SERVER=127.0.0.1 in the Linux environment (the ADL audio guide and adl-configure-audio.sh do this).
issues.securityImpact: The TCP module is restricted to 127.0.0.1 (device-local only); do not widen the ACL.

issues.title: PulseAudio output reported broken on Android 16
symptoms: Audio forwarding configured correctly but no sound on Android 16
issues.cause: Open upstream report of the Termux PulseAudio OpenSL ES sink failing on Android 16; not yet resolved at last verification.
diagnostics: Confirm the same setup works on an Android ≤15 device; check the upstream issue for status
issues.workaround: Track the upstream issue; wired/Bluetooth routing changes may help in some reports.

issues.title: Linux session starts as the (simulated) root user by default
symptoms: whoami prints root after proot-distro login
Browsers refuse to start or warn about running as root
issues.cause: proot-distro logs in as the container's root user unless --user is passed. This is simulated root inside the container, not Android root.
issues.safeFix: Create a normal user and log in with 'proot-distro login <distro> --user <name>' (the guide and adl-create-user.sh do this).

issues.title: Chromium fails to start under proot (sandbox / GPU process)
symptoms: 'Running as root without --no-sandbox is not supported'
Chromium window never appears or crashes at startup
issues.cause: Chromium's sandbox needs user namespaces that proot cannot provide as root; its GPU process also fails on the software GL stack.
issues.safeFix: Use Firefox (Debian firefox-esr or Mozilla's apt repo on Ubuntu). If you must try Chromium, run it as a non-root user — never adopt --no-sandbox as a default.
issues.securityImpact: --no-sandbox removes the browser's main security boundary; ADL does not recommend it.

issues.title: Firefox misrenders or crashes until hardware acceleration is disabled
symptoms: Black or corrupted page content
Firefox crashes on startup or when opening pages
issues.cause: The GL stack under proot + Termux:X11 is software-only and unusual; Firefox's hardware acceleration path can fail on it.
issues.safeFix: Settings > General > Performance: uncheck 'Use recommended performance settings', then uncheck 'Use hardware acceleration when available'. Or set gfx.webrender.software=true in about:config. Restart Firefox.

sources.title: Fedora wiki — Firefox hardware acceleration prefs

issues.title: Ubuntu's firefox/chromium packages are Snap stubs that cannot run under proot
symptoms: apt install firefox succeeds but Firefox does not start
Package description says 'Transitional package - firefox -> firefox snap'
issues.cause: Ubuntu ships browser debs that only install a Snap, and snapd cannot run under proot.
issues.safeFix: Install Firefox from Mozilla's official apt repository (packages.mozilla.org, serves arm64), or use Debian with firefox-esr.

sources.title: Ubuntu noble firefox — transitional snap package
Mozilla apt repo Release file (arm64 listed)

issues.title: Termux:X11 shows a black screen
symptoms: Black screen with cursor
Black screen without cursor
issues.cause: Desktop session not started, DISPLAY not set, missing --shared-tmp, or a device-specific drawing issue.
diagnostics: Was the session started with the correct DISPLAY (:1)?
issues.safeFix: Start the session as 'termux-x11 :1 -xstartup "dbus-launch --exit-with-session xfce4-session"' and log in with --shared-tmp. If still black, try the -legacy-drawing flag (documented for this symptom).

sources.title: termux-x11 README — flags and shared tmp requirement

issues.title: Termux:X11 shows swapped red/blue colors
symptoms: Everything renders with red and blue channels exchanged
issues.safeFix: Start termux-x11 with the -force-bgra flag (documented for exactly this symptom). Do not apply it preemptively.

issues.title: Tiny text / wrong scaling on high-DPI screens
symptoms: Desktop renders with unreadably small text on the phone screen
issues.safeFix: Pass '-dpi 120' (or higher) to termux-x11, and/or raise the desktop's own scaling settings (Xfce: Settings > Appearance > Fonts > Custom DPI).

issues.title: Desktop session fails with D-Bus errors
symptoms: 'Failed to connect to session bus'
Session exits immediately after starting
issues.cause: Desktops expect a session D-Bus; under proot it must be started with the session (dbus-launch) and the dbus package must be installed.
issues.safeFix: Install dbus (and dbus-x11 on Debian/Ubuntu) in the distro and start the session via 'dbus-launch --exit-with-session <session-command>'.

issues.title: GNOME does not work under proot
symptoms: gnome-session fails to start or immediately exits
Errors about logind/systemd user session
issues.cause: GNOME hard-depends on logind and (since GNOME 49) the systemd user service manager; GNOME 50 removed X11 session support entirely. proot provides none of these.
issues.safeFix: Use Xfce, MATE, LXQt, or KDE Plasma (X11) instead. ADL documents GNOME as unsupported under proot.

issues.title: KDE Plasma is removing its X11 session (Plasma 6.8)
symptoms: Future distro releases shipping Plasma 6.8+ will have no startplasma-x11
issues.cause: KDE announced Plasma 6.8 (expected Oct 2026) ships Wayland-only; Plasma ≤6.7 keeps the X11 session. Current Debian stable (Plasma 6.3) is unaffected.
issues.workaround: Stay on distro releases with Plasma ≤6.7 for X11 sessions, or choose Xfce/MATE.

sources.title: KDE blog — Wayland future / X11 session removal

issues.title: Installation fails partway with 'No space left on device'
symptoms: apt/pkg errors mentioning disk space
Partial desktop install that will not start
issues.cause: Desktop installations download and unpack several gigabytes; running out mid-install leaves a broken package state.
diagnostics: df -h in Termux and inside the distro
issues.safeFix: Free space, then run 'apt --fix-broken install' (Debian/Ubuntu) and retry. The wizard's storage estimate includes a safety reserve to avoid this.

issues.title: Package manager lock after an interrupted install
symptoms: 'Could not get lock /var/lib/dpkg/lock-frontend'
apt refuses to run
issues.cause: A previous apt/dpkg run was interrupted (often by Android killing the process).
issues.safeFix: Make sure no apt process is running (pgrep apt), then run 'dpkg --configure -a' followed by 'apt --fix-broken install'. Do not delete lock files as a first step.

issues.title: Android intercepts keyboard shortcuts meant for Linux
symptoms: Alt-Tab, Home, or other shortcuts act on Android instead of the desktop
issues.cause: Android's system shortcuts take priority over the Termux:X11 window on some devices/keyboards.
issues.workaround: Remap conflicting shortcuts inside the desktop, use the Termux:X11 fullscreen preference, or change Samsung DeX keyboard settings where applicable.
